title: Loupe Blueprint
description: Pre-dispatch structured plan of what a coding agent would change to resolve a GitHub issue. Produced by any provider implementing the PlanningProvider interface.

outcome.description: ready = blueprint produced, agent can be dispatched. needs_info = ticket missing prerequisites, dispatch blocked.

missing_info.description: Required when outcome=needs_info. Specific items the ticket needs before it can be planned.

summary.description: One-sentence summary of the proposed change.

size.description: Rough scope estimate: S=trivial, M=routine, L=multi-file, XL=cross-cutting

path.description: Repo-relative path. For new files, a provisional path the agent may rename.

is_new.description: True if this file does not currently exist.

why.description: Reason this file is expected to change.

deps.description: New dependencies the change is likely to need. Only include after verifying the manifest does not already contain them.

migrations.description: Schema/data mutations the change is likely to need.

risk_areas.description: Standardized risk vocabulary. Empty array if no specific risk surface.

out_of_scope.description: Files the agent should explicitly NOT modify even if they seem related.

open_questions.description: Genuine blocking decisions only. DEFAULT TO EMPTY — most tickets have zero. Add a question only if not answering it would force a choice between materially different implementations that a competent engineer would stop and ask about. Never about style/naming/formatting or anything inferable from the repo. If non-empty, blueprint_confidence must be correspondingly lower.

default_agent.description: Recommended harness for this ticket character.

blueprint_confidence.description: Honest belief the agent will succeed on first attempt without send-back. Below 0.5 = human should plan first.
